channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar on Friday, bringing down a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok and damaging historic buildings in Myanmar.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under U.N.-sponsored external governance, boasts battlefield gains
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to put Ukraine under external governance under the U.N. aegis as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, a blustery statement that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under U.N.-sponsored external governance, boasts battlefield gains
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has proposed putting Ukraine under external governance under the U.N. aegis as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Burma, Thailand causing buildings to collapse
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Burma and Thailand midday Friday, causing buildings to collapse, and sending people scrambling for their lives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, killing at least 2 in Bangkok high-rise collapse
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, killing at least two people as a high-rise building under construction in Bangkok collapsed, while hundreds of thousands were forced to evacuate from their homes and workplaces.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A remote Australian town seeks a doctor, offering a $400,000 salary and free rent
item.description: A remote Australian town that will soon lose its only doctor is offering a salary package of up to $680,000 Australian dollars, plus free rent and a car, to attract a new candidate.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US defense chief says stronger deterrence needed against China in disputed sea
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Friday that the Trump administration would boost military ties with the Philippines to strengthen deterrence against “threats from the communist Chinese” and ensure freedom of navigation in the disputed South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trial of former President Sarkozy sheds light on France’s back-channel talks with Libya’s Gadhafi
item.description: The trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign is shedding light on France’s back-channel talks with the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes have pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the country’s Houthi rebels, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Knife-wielding man injures multiple people in Amsterdam, including 2 Americans
item.description: Dutch police are searching for a motive after five people, including two Americans, were stabbed in a busy shopping area of Amsterdam on Thursday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: Charles' cancer diagnosis has heaped pressure on the British monarchy, which is still evolving after the 70-year reign of the late Queen Elizabeth II.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK doesn't want to 'escalate' Trump trade war with auto tariff response
item.description: British Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves warned against heightening tensions with President Trump during his self-declared trade war against nations that oppose his “America First” policy. “We're not at the moment in a position where we want to do anything to escalate these trade wars.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US ramps up demands on Ukraine in latest mineral proposal
item.description: President Trump’s administration is ramping up demands on Ukraine in the latest proposal, looking to assume control of all of Kyiv’s critical energy and mineral resources as it pushes ahead to reach a ceasefire in the country’s three-year war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: French president says not all European allies agree on a proposed force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France and Britain will continue to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops in Ukraine to defend an eventual peace deal with Russia but only some other nations want to take part, French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday after a summit of countries that have been mulling the propos
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SAMUEL PETREQUIN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Danish TV reporter: Americans knocked on doors in Greenland's capital; couldn't find hosts
item.description: A Danish television reporter said American officials knocked on doors in Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, seeking local hosts for second lady Usha Vance's planned visit, but they largely got a cold shoulder.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Brazilian President Lula swipes at Trump as US imposes auto tariffs
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva swiped Thursday at U.S. President Donald Trump's decision to raise U.S. tariffs on a wide range of products, saying Trump should consider the potential harm to the American and global economies.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim oversees test of AI-powered suicide drones
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of newly developed AI-powered suicide drones and called for their increased production, North Korean state media said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: ASSISI, Italy (AP) — Pilgrims have been pouring into this medieval hilltop town to venerate not only two of the Catholic Church’s most celebrated saints, Francis and Clare, but its newest — Carlo Acutis, the first millennial saint, who will be canonized on April 27. “St. Francis, St.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Houthis claim responsibility for strikes against US ships: report
item.description: The Houthis in Yemen claimed responsibility for alleged recent attacks against the USS Harry S. Truman aircraft carrier and U.S. warships in the Red Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, after 38 days battling a life-threatening case of pneumonia in both lungs, his doctors said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Heathrow says some flights will resume after a fire cut power to Europe's busiest airport
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heathrow Airport said it planned to resume some flights Friday after a large fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to Europe’s busiest flight hub and disrupted global travel for hundreds of thousands of passengers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, DANICA KIRKA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice for travel to the United States on Thursday, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: European countries warn against US travel amid immigration crackdown
item.description: The UK and Germany updated travel advisories after citizens were arrested and detained when attempting to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation
